metadata.vintage: Confirmed from CHFA's official Dates and Deadlines page (chfainfo.com/rental-housing/housing-credit/dates-and-deadlines) on 2026-06-03. CHFA's published cycle: R1 = 9% + State Credit (Feb deadline); R2 = 4% + State Credit (Aug deadline). Pure 4% deals are rolling; R2 specifically targets 4%-with-State-Credit projects. MIHTC has its own annual deadline.
notes: Round 1 = 9% LIHTC + State Credit (highly competitive; February application)
Round 2 = 4% LIHTC + State Credit (4% paired with State Credit; August application). Pure 4% / PAB is rolling outside this cycle.
MIHTC (Middle Income Housing Tax Credit) is annual with its own LOI/app cycle (March/May)
Every CHFA round requires a Letter of Intent (LOI) 60+ days before the application — LOI deadlines are the real first gate
Prop 123 awards run through DOLA on a separate cycle — see cdola.colorado.gov/proposition-123
Annual Allocation Plan published in December for following calendar year
QAP draft + public comment period typically September-October each year

events.name: 2026 R1 (9% + State) — Letter of Intent due
events.details: LOI was required 60+ days before R1 application.

events.name: 2026 R1 9% LIHTC + State Credit — application deadline
events.details: R1 applications were due Feb 2, 2026 by 5pm MT. 14 developments totaling 634 units / $219M allocated.

events.name: 2026 R1 9% LIHTC — awards announced
events.details: R1 reservation announcements; 2026 R1 awards bridge file in data/affordable-housing/chfa-awards/2026-round-one.json until live ArcGIS catches up.

events.name: 2026 MIHTC — Letter of Intent due
events.details: LOI for state Middle Income Housing Tax Credit was due March 16, 2026 by 5pm MT.

events.name: 2026 MIHTC — application deadline
events.details: Middle Income Housing Tax Credit applications were due May 18, 2026 by 5pm MT.

events.name: 2026 R2 (4% + State) — Letter of Intent due
events.details: LOI for R2 (4% LIHTC paired with State Credit) was due June 1, 2026 by 5pm MT — the required gate 60+ days before the August application.

events.name: 2026 R2 4% LIHTC + State Credit — application deadline
events.details: R2 applications due August 3, 2026 by 5pm MT. R2 is the bond-driven 4% LIHTC paired with the Colorado State Credit (not 9% — that's R1). Pure 4% PAB-only deals stay on a rolling cycle outside this round.

events.name: 2026 R2 4% LIHTC + State Credit — awards announced
events.details: R2 reservations typically announced mid-December. Bridge file pattern in data/affordable-housing/chfa-awards/ will be used until live ArcGIS catches up.

events.name: 2027 QAP draft — public comment period
events.details: Draft 2027 Qualified Allocation Plan typically released for public comment in September-October. Critical window for developers + advocates to flag scoring concerns.

events.name: 2027 Annual Allocation Plan published
events.details: CHFA's 2027 Annual Allocation Plan typically published in December. Sets cycle dates, credit ceiling, set-asides for the upcoming year.

events.name: 2027 R1 (9% + State) — Letter of Intent due
events.details: Estimated based on the 2026 R1 LOI cadence (LOI due 60+ days before application). LOI is the operative gate — without it on file, you cannot submit the R1 application. Verify exact date once CHFA publishes the 2027 calendar.

events.name: 2027 R1 9% LIHTC + State Credit — application deadline
events.details: Estimated based on CHFA's historical early-February R1 deadline (R1 = 9% + State Credit). LOI prerequisite is due ~Dec 1, 2026 — see the LOI event. Verify exact date on CHFA's Dates and Deadlines page once 2027 calendar is published.

events.name: 2027 R1 9% LIHTC — awards announced
events.details: R1 reservation announcements typically late May.

rolling_programs.name: 4% LIHTC + PAB (Private Activity Bond)
rolling_programs.description: 4% Tax-Exempt Bond financing is reviewed on a rolling basis (no fixed deadline). Limited by annual PAB allocation — see data/pab-allocations.json for current year's available cap.

rolling_programs.name: MIHTC (Middle Income Housing Tax Credit)
rolling_programs.description: State-only Middle Income tax credit serving 80-120% AMI. Annual cycle typically R1-aligned. Small allocation; competitive.

rolling_programs.name: Colorado State LIHTC
rolling_programs.description: State tax credit MUST be paired with federal 9% or 4%. Same cycles as federal. Roughly doubles equity yield on stacked deals.

rolling_programs.name: Proposition 123 — Affordable Housing Fund
rolling_programs.description: Colorado statutory affordable-housing fund administered by DOLA. Separate cycle from CHFA — see cdola.colorado.gov/proposition-123 for current rounds.
